title: The Grand Mosalla at Noon

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: A day held a notch below neutral — a Tehran funeral, a besieged city, an escalating war and a record heat dome pressing against markets near highs, a pope's plea for the drowned, and a run of quiet science wins.

style.genre: noir spy-jazz / cinematic-mystery — a brushed AkaiLinn kit walking soft under a plucked upright bass and a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling F harmonic minor topline, a muted clean electric-guitar line answering in the gaps, a low bowed-string pad holding the dread and a warm sine sub far below, all through deep tape-echo; harmonic minor's raised seventh dragging hard against its lowered sixth — the exact half-step tension of a day that will not resolve either way

sampleSources: Strudel default library — VCSL vibraphone & strings, sampled electric guitar, AkaiLinn drum machine
pattern:
stack(
  s("~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ [hh hh], bd ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~ ~ rim").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.52).room(0.22).swingBy(0.12, 4),
  note("<f2 ~ c3 df3 g2 ~ c3 e3>").s("sawtooth").lpf(520).attack(0.01).decay(0.18).sustain(0.35).release(0.2).gain(0.42),
  note("<[f2,af2,c3] [c3,ef3,g3] [df3,f3,af3] [c3,e3,g3]>").s("gm_acoustic_bass").room(0.25).gain(0.4).lpf(1600),
  note("<[f3,af3,c4,e4] [df3,f3,af3,c4] [g3,bf3,df4] [c4,e4,g4]>").s("vibraphone").gain(0.5).room(0.4).rsize(4).lpf(sine.range(1400,3200).slow(12)).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.375),
  note("f4 ~ g4 af4 ~ e4 f4 ~ ~ c5 bf4 af4 ~ g4 ~ f4").s("vibraphone").gain(0.34).room(0.5).delay(0.32).delaytime(0.25).pan(0.6).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.16).pan(0.3)),
  note("<~ ~ [af4 g4] ~>").s("gm_electric_guitar_muted").gain(0.3).lpf(2400).room(0.35).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.5).pan(0.35),
  note("<f2 c3 df3 c3>").s("sawtooth").lpf(220).attack(0.02).sustain(0.9).release(1.2).gain(0.3).room(0.3)
)

lines: Noon at the Grand Mosalla, a flag laid over the box,
a week of processions counted in millions, and the talks stay locked in a pause —
half a million behind the siege wall waiting on the dark,
and the drones go up over Crimea like a forty-day-long spark.
So the needle sits at forty-three, a hair below the line,
the heat dome over the fireworks, the market holding fine —
nothing rises, nothing breaks, it just refuses to resolve,
an uneasy calm that hums all day and asks us to absolve.
The Pope walks Lampedusa's pier and prays above the drowned,
receive them, protect them, integrate — a small clear human sound;
a cell learns how to burn a tumor, a magnon holds its light,
eight hundred million lamps switched on somewhere against the night.
